clinicalLanguage.disclaimer: A symptom-pattern screening score is not a diagnosis, a medical probability, or confirmation of any condition. It only prioritizes which functional patterns to review with your practitioner.

questions.text: Do you experience unexplained weight gain or difficulty losing weight?
Do you feel cold when others feel comfortable?
Do you have dry skin, brittle nails, or thinning hair?
Do you experience constipation regularly?
Do you feel sluggish or fatigued, especially in the morning?
Do you have brain fog or poor concentration?
Do you have a puffy face or swelling around eyes?
Do you experience muscle weakness or aches?
Do you have irregular or heavy menstrual periods (if applicable)?
Do you have elevated cholesterol despite healthy diet?

categories.name: Adrenal Function
categories.description: Adrenal fatigue and HPA axis symptoms

questions.text: Do you feel "wired but tired" - exhausted yet unable to relax?
Do you need caffeine or stimulants to get through the day?
Do you feel dizzy or lightheaded when standing up quickly?
Do you crave salt or salty foods?
Do you have a "second wind" of energy late at night (after 11pm)?
Do you feel overwhelmed by stress that you previously handled well?
Do you have difficulty recovering from illness or exercise?
Do you experience afternoon energy crashes (2-4pm)?
Do you startle easily or feel anxious without reason?
Do you have low blood pressure or feel faint?

categories.name: Hormonal Balance
categories.description: Sex hormone and metabolic hormone symptoms

questions.text: Do you experience low libido or sexual dysfunction?
Do you have mood swings, irritability, or depression?
Do you experience hot flashes or night sweats?
Do you have difficulty building or maintaining muscle?
Do you have acne, oily skin, or excess facial hair (women)?
Do you experience PMS, irregular periods, or painful periods?
Do you have weight gain around hips, thighs, or midsection?
Do you feel fatigued despite adequate sleep?
Do you have breast tenderness or fibrocystic breasts?
Do you experience water retention or bloating cyclically?

categories.name: Digestive Health
categories.description: SIBO, leaky gut, IBS, Crohn's symptoms

questions.text: Do you experience bloating within 1-2 hours after eating?
Do you have excessive gas or flatulence?
Do you experience alternating constipation and diarrhea?
Do you have abdominal pain or cramping?
Do you notice undigested food in your stool?
Do you react to many foods that didn't bother you before?
Do you experience acid reflux or heartburn?
Do you have mucus or blood in your stool?
Do symptoms worsen with fiber, prebiotics, or probiotics?
Do you feel worse after eating fermented foods?
Do you have difficulty digesting fatty or greasy foods?
Do you experience pain or discomfort under right ribcage?
Do you have nausea after eating fatty meals?
Do you have light-colored or floating stools?
Do you experience burping or belching after meals?
Do you have pain between shoulder blades?
Do you feel sluggish or tired after eating?
Do you have a history of gallstones or gallbladder removal?
Do you experience bitter taste in mouth?
Do you have difficulty absorbing fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K)?

categories.name: Blood Sugar
categories.description: Blood sugar and insulin resistance symptoms

categories.name: Chronic Viruses (EBV)
categories.description: EBV, CMV, and chronic viral reactivation symptoms

questions.text: Have you ever had mononucleosis (mono/glandular fever)?
Do you have swollen lymph nodes in neck or armpits?
Do you experience chronic sore throat or recurring infections?
Do you have extreme fatigue that started after an illness?
Do you experience low-grade fevers or feeling feverish?
Do you have muscle or joint pain without injury?
Do symptoms worsen with physical exertion (post-exertional malaise)?
Do you have recurring cold sores or shingles outbreaks?
Do you experience unrefreshing sleep despite adequate hours?
Do you have brain fog or cognitive issues that fluctuate?
Do you have sensitivity to supplements, medications, or chemicals?
Do you experience anxiety, depression, or mood instability?
Do you have high homocysteine levels (if tested)?
Do you react poorly to B vitamins, especially B12 or folate?
Do you have a family history of heart disease, stroke, or dementia?
Do you experience brain fog or difficulty concentrating?
Do you have histamine intolerance symptoms (flushing, hives, headaches)?
Do you have chronic fatigue not explained by other causes?
Do you have fertility issues or history of miscarriage?

Do you experience headaches or pressure near electronics or WiFi?

Do you experience ringing in ears (tinnitus)?
Do you feel fatigued or irritable after extended screen time?
Do you have heart palpitations near electrical equipment?
Do you experience skin tingling or burning sensations?
Do symptoms improve when in nature away from electronics?

Do you experience dizziness or nausea near cell towers or power lines?
Have you noticed worsening symptoms with 5G or new technology?

Do you experience skin issues (acne, eczema, rashes)?
Do you have joint pain or inflammation?

labRules.notes: Rules fire when a category screening score reaches the moderate band (>=25). Elevated (>=50) raises rank, never adds panels. Derived from the legacy insights.tsx mapping; clinical review pending.

rules.why: Assesses liver and gallbladder function markers

Full microbiome analysis with pathogen and dysbiosis markers

Includes fasting glucose, HbA1c, insulin, and metabolic markers

Identifies IgG reactions that may accompany autoimmune responses

Includes parasite detection and gut pathogen analysis
Urinary mycotoxin testing for mold exposure
Heavy metal panel including lead, mercury, arsenic, cadmium
Mercury speciation for dental amalgam and fish exposure
Genetic analysis including MTHFR, COMT, and detox pathways
Includes EBV, CMV, HHV-6, and other chronic viral markers
Professional EMF assessment of home and work environment (not a lab test)

consents.title: Privacy & data use
consents.summary: How your health information is stored, protected, and used to provide care. No diagnosis is made from screening questionnaires.

consents.title: Communications
consents.summary: Secure portal messaging is the default channel. You choose whether reminders may use other channels.

consents.title: Telehealth
consents.summary: Applies when you book video visits: technology limits, privacy, and emergency guidance.

consents.title: Clinical care relationship
consents.summary: Screening scores and drafts are reviewed by your practitioner before anything becomes a recommendation or order.

consents.title: Visit recording & AI assistance
consents.summary: Optional, revocable consent for visit recording and AI-drafted notes; nothing is enabled without it.
